docs.title: API Keys
docs.text: Create scoped API keys for apps and integrations.

docs.title: Create a key
docs.text:
1) Open Admin → API Keys 2) Click “Create key” and choose scopes (e.g., fax:send, fax:read) 3) Copy the generated token (fbk_live_<id>_<secret>) — it’s shown once

Scopes

Use least‑privilege: sending apps need fax:send; dashboards often need fax:read only.

docs.title: Use in requests
docs.text: curl -H "X-API-Key: fbk_live_..." "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax/{id}"

docs.title: Rotate and revoke
docs.text:
• Rotate keys periodically and revoke unused ones
  • Jobs remain readable if the user/account retains access

docs.title: Diagnostics
docs.text: Run safe connectivity checks and review provider‑specific tips.

docs.title: Tools
docs.text:
• Health: DB/storage/backends readiness
  • Providers: DNS, auth reachability, webhook URL reachability
  • Tunnels: quick tunnel status, logs tail
What’s checked

Checks derive from provider traits (e.g., inbound_verification, needs_storage). Results include remediation links.

docs.title: Actions
docs.text:
• Re‑probe a single provider
  • View recommended callback URLs
  • Copy troubleshooting links

See also - Networking & Tunnels - Webhooks

docs.title: Plugin Builder
docs.text: Install, build, and upload provider plugins — including inbound providers — without leaving the Admin Console.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
• Browse available plugins and click Install
  • Upload a signed manifest to add custom providers

docs.title: Configure
docs.text:
• Provider fields and help links render from the plugin manifest
  • Inbound plugins expose a webhook path and verification mode (HMAC/Basic) and surface a copyable URL

SignalWire inbound

Add a SignalWire inbound plugin here to enable inbound fax delivery. After install, register the shown webhook URL in SignalWire.

docs.title: Test
docs.text:
• Run plugin diagnostics from Admin → Diagnostics
  • Use “Simulate inbound” where available to verify storage and Inbox

docs.title: Settings
docs.text: Manage outbound/inbound providers, storage, and security.

docs.title: Providers
docs.text:
OutboundInbound

Select your sending backend. Cloud providers expose credential fields and status callbacks; self‑hosted backends show AMI/T.38 guidance.

Choose an inbound provider or install one via Plugins. The UI shows the exact webhook URL and verification method with copy‑button.

Traits‑driven UI

The Settings screen renders fields from provider traits — no backend name checks. This guarantees clean isolation and correct guidance.

docs.title: Storage
docs.text:
• Local or S3‑compatible storage for inbound PDFs and artifacts
  • Short‑TTL tokens for PDF access; scope‑guarded downloads

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
• API Key enforcement (X-API-Key)
  • HMAC verification for supported providers; Basic auth for others
  • Optional OAuth/OIDC for Admin login

See also - Webhooks - Diagnostics

docs.title: Setup Wizard
docs.text:
Configure Faxbot end‑to‑end from a guided flow: outbound provider, optional inbound, storage, and security.

Quick links

Settings Diagnostics Plugin Builder

Evaluate (Quick Tunnel)Production (HIPAA)

1) Choose a cloud provider (Phaxio, Sinch, SignalWire, Documo, HumbleFax) 2) Enter credentials and click Validate 3) Open Tools → Tunnels and start a quick tunnel to get a public URL 4) Set PUBLIC_API_URL and register webhooks where supported

1) Execute a BAA and disable provider document retention (cloud) 2) Use a named tunnel or domain (HTTPS) 3) Enable inbound with the correct verification (HMAC/Basic) 4) Rotate keys and restrict Admin access

Backend isolation

The wizard shows provider‑specific fields only for the selected backend. Links and help are scoped; nothing is mixed across providers.

Next steps

  • Settings
  • Diagnostics
  • Plugins

docs.title: Apps
docs.text:
Companion apps for using Faxbot beyond the browser.

  • iOS: Pair with your server and access Inbox on the go
  • Desktop (Electron): Native shell around the Admin Console

Commercial notice

The iOS app is part of the commercial offering. These docs cover usage and pairing only.

Quick links

iOS :material-desktop-mac: Desktop

docs.title: Desktop (Electron)
docs.text: A native shell around the Admin Console for desktop workflows.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
1) Launch the app and point it at your Faxbot server (e.g., https://your-domain) 2) Sign in with an Admin API key 3) Use the Admin Console tabs for Setup, Settings, Jobs, Inbox, and Diagnostics

Security

The renderer runs sandboxed; no Node APIs are exposed to web content. Keep your server on HTTPS and never expose Admin Terminal over public tunnels.

See also - Admin Console: Settings - Admin Console: Diagnostics

docs.title: iOS App
docs.text: Pair your device with Faxbot to view Inbox and receive notifications.

docs.title: Pairing
docs.text:
1) Ensure your server is reachable from the device (named tunnel or domain) 2) Admin → Mobile Pairing → Generate code (or QR) 3) In the iOS app, enter the code (or scan the QR) 4) The app exchanges the code for a short‑lived token and the best base URL

Tunnels

For off‑LAN access, use a HIPAA‑capable tunnel (WireGuard/Tailscale). Avoid quick tunnels for PHI.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• Code expired → generate a new one
  • Cannot connect → verify the tunnel/domain is reachable over HTTPS from your device
  • Inbox empty → verify inbound provider and webhooks are configured

See also - Networking & Tunnels - Inbound & Webhooks

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
Launch Faxbot locally, send your first fax, and explore the Admin Console.

Quick links

Setup Wizard Go‑Live Webhooks

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
1) Start the stack (Docker or local) and open the Admin Console 2) Run Setup Wizard to select a backend and validate credentials 3) Send a test fax from the UI or API

curlAdmin Console 
curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -F to=+15551234567 -F file=@README.md | jq .

Use the Send Fax panel, upload a PDF/TXT, and submit.

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Go‑Live Checklists
  • Networking & Tunnels
  • Webhooks

docs.title: Go‑Live Checklists
docs.text:
Final readiness checklists for each backend profile. Each page is backend‑specific and includes provider help links, webhook guidance, and HIPAA notes where applicable.

Quick links

Networking & Tunnels Webhooks Setup Wizard

Tip

Use search [] with Ctrl+K (or ⌘+K) to jump between providers and guides quickly.

docs.title: Providers
docs.text:
• Phaxio
  • Sinch
  • SignalWire
  • Documo (mFax)
  • HumbleFax
  • SIP/Asterisk
  • FreeSWITCH

Auto‑tunnel and webhooks

Evaluating without a public domain? Use Admin Console → Tools → Tunnels to create a Cloudflare Quick Tunnel (non‑HIPAA) and auto‑detect your public URL. Then set PUBLIC_API_URL and register webhooks (where supported) for your selected provider.

HIPAA posture

For HIPAA workloads, avoid quick tunnels. Use a secure tunnel like WireGuard or Tailscale, ensure HTTPS termination, disable provider document retention, and execute a BAA with the provider. See HIPAA Requirements.

docs.title: Print‑ready one‑pagers
docs.text:
• Phaxio — One‑Pager
  • Sinch — One‑Pager
  • SIP/Asterisk — One‑Pager
  • HumbleFax — One‑Pager
  • Documo — One‑Pager

See also: Networking & Tunnels · Webhooks · Admin Console Setup Wizard

docs.title: Documo (mFax) — One‑Pager
docs.text: Direct upload with Documo’s mFax API.

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] DOCUMO_API_KEY
  • [ ] Optional: DOCUMO_BASE_URL, DOCUMO_USE_SANDBOX=true
  • [ ] Admin Console → Backend: documo
  • [ ] API_KEY minted; clients send X-API-Key
  • [ ] PUBLIC_API_URL set for Admin links

docs.title: Test
docs.text:
curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -F to=+15551234567 -F file=@README.md | jq .

docs.title: Help
docs.text:
• Documo API docs
  • mFax pricing & signup

HIPAA

Confirm provider retention policies and execute a BAA before handling PHI. See HIPAA Requirements.

docs.title: Documo (mFax) Go‑Live Checklist
docs.text:
Documo’s mFax API is a cloud backend with direct upload. PUBLIC_API_URL is not required for outbound, but is recommended for Admin links.

Quick links

API Docs mFax Pricing Setup Wizard

docs.title: Pre‑flight
docs.text:
• [ ] Documo API Key
  • [ ] Environment: DOCUMO_API_KEY, optional DOCUMO_BASE_URL, optional DOCUMO_USE_SANDBOX=true
  • [ ] Admin Console → Settings → Backend: documo
  • [ ] Set API_KEY and ensure clients send X-API-Key
  • [ ] PUBLIC_API_URL set for Admin links

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Outbound uses direct upload; status is derived from provider responses
  • Inbound not supported (use a different inbound provider if needed)

Helpful docs - Documo API docs - mFax pricing & signup

docs.title: Smoke tests
docs.text:
Send a test fax via Faxbot

curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -F "to=+15551234567" \
  -F "file=@README.md" | jq .

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• 401 from Faxbot → set API_KEY and send X-API-Key
  • Job not created → verify Production vs Sandbox selection; check API key validity
  • 413 / size errors → adjust MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB; only PDF/TXT accepted

HIPAA posture

Confirm provider retention policies and execute a BAA before handling PHI. Review HIPAA Requirements.

docs.title: FreeSWITCH Go‑Live Checklist
docs.text: Self‑hosted backend using FreeSWITCH. Requires a SIP trunk and correct T.38 support via mod_spandsp.

docs.title: Pre‑flight
docs.text:
• [ ] FreeSWITCH running with mod_spandsp enabled
  • [ ] SIP trunk registered and reachable (sofia/gateway/<name>)
  • [ ] T.38 configured where possible; fall back to G.711 if needed
  • [ ] Admin Console → Settings → Backend: freeswitch
  • [ ] Inbound: not supported (use a cloud inbound provider if needed)
  • [ ] PUBLIC_API_URL set for Admin links (not required for telephony)

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Outbound originates via your FreeSWITCH gateway
  • No inbound webhooks; inbound support is not available in this backend

Helpful docs - FreeSWITCH Setup (Faxbot) - FreeSWITCH Project

docs.title: Smoke tests
docs.text:
• Place a test outbound fax through your FreeSWITCH gateway
curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -F "to=+15551234567" \
  -F "file=@README.md" | jq .

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• Negotiation errors → check T.38 SDP, gateway configuration, and codec settings
  • Jobs stuck → verify gateway registration and outbound route permissions

HIPAA posture

Keep SIP and media private. Use WireGuard/Tailscale for remote access to Admin only, and review HIPAA Requirements.

docs.title: HumbleFax — One‑Pager
docs.text: Quick steps for outbound + inbound with HumbleFax.

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] HUMBLEFAX_ACCESS_KEY, HUMBLEFAX_SECRET_KEY
  • [ ] Optional: HUMBLEFAX_FROM_NUMBER
  • [ ] Admin Console → Backend: humblefax
  • [ ] PUBLIC_API_URL (HTTPS)
  • [ ] Webhook: <PUBLIC_API_URL>/inbound/humblefax/webhook
  • [ ] Optional: HUMBLEFAX_WEBHOOK_SECRET (HMAC)
  • [ ] API_KEY minted; clients send X-API-Key

docs.title: Test
docs.text:
curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -F to=+15551234567 -F file=@README.md | jq .

docs.title: Help
docs.text:
• How it works
  • Sign up
  • Dashboard

HIPAA

HumbleFax may not be HIPAA‑compliant. For PHI, choose a HIPAA‑capable provider and follow HIPAA Requirements.

docs.title: HumbleFax Go‑Live Checklist
docs.text:
HumbleFax is a lightweight cloud provider supported for outbound sending and inbound delivery via webhooks. It also offers a native email‑to‑fax flow you can use alongside Faxbot.

Quick links

How it works Sign up Setup Wizard

docs.title: Pre‑flight
docs.text:
• [ ] HumbleFax Access Key and Secret Key
  • [ ] Optional default From number (HUMBLEFAX_FROM_NUMBER)
  • [ ] Admin Console → Settings → Backend: humblefax
  • [ ] Set API_KEY and ensure clients send X-API-Key
  • [ ] PUBLIC_API_URL points to an HTTPS host
  • [ ] Inbound webhooks configured to <PUBLIC_API_URL>/inbound/humblefax/webhook
  • [ ] Optional: HUMBLEFAX_WEBHOOK_SECRET set for HMAC verification

One‑shot tunnel + webhook register

Use scripts/setup-humblefax-tunnel.sh to start a tunnel, detect the public URL, set HUMBLEFAX_CALLBACK_BASE, and register the webhook automatically.

docs.title: Webhooks
docs.text:
• Outbound status: SentFax events update job status by provider_sid
  • Inbound delivery: IncomingFax events trigger background PDF download into your configured storage backend
  • Verification: HMAC‑SHA256 when HUMBLEFAX_WEBHOOK_SECRET is set (header X-Humblefax-Signature)

Helpful links - HumbleFax – How it works - HumbleFax – Sign up - HumbleFax – Dashboard

docs.title: Email → Fax (native HumbleFax)
docs.text: Send an email to <fax-number>@humblefax.com with your attachments. Subject/body become a cover sheet. This works independently of Faxbot and is useful for quick wins.

docs.title: Smoke tests
docs.text:
Send a test fax via Faxbot

curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -F "to=+15551234567" \
  -F "file=@README.md" | jq .

Diagnostics

curl -sS -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  "$PUBLIC_API_URL/admin/diagnostics/humblefax" | jq .

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• Webhook URL not reachable → set PUBLIC_API_URL (HTTPS) or use a stable tunnel
  • Auth failures → verify access/secret keys and optional From number
  • No inbound PDFs → ensure storage backend is configured and reachable

HIPAA posture

HumbleFax may not be HIPAA‑compliant. If you handle PHI, choose a HIPAA‑capable provider and follow HIPAA Requirements.

docs.title: Phaxio Go‑Live — One‑Pager
docs.text: Essentials for a clean Phaxio launch.

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] PHAXIO_API_KEY and PHAXIO_API_SECRET set
  • [ ] Admin Console → Backend: phaxio
  • [ ] PUBLIC_API_URL (HTTPS) reachable from the internet
  • [ ] Phaxio status callback → <PUBLIC_API_URL>/phaxio-callback
  • [ ] PHAXIO_VERIFY_SIGNATURE=true (HMAC)
  • [ ] API_KEY minted; clients send X-API-Key
  • [ ] HIPAA: BAA executed; provider retention disabled

docs.title: Test
docs.text:
curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -F to=+15551234567 -F file=@README.md | jq .

docs.title: Troubleshoot
docs.text:
• No callbacks → verify HTTPS and callback URL in Phaxio console
  • Signature fail → check PHAXIO_VERIFY_SIGNATURE and secret
  • 415 errors → only PDF/TXT accepted

docs.title: HIPAA checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] BAA executed with provider
  • [ ] Provider document retention disabled
  • [ ] HTTPS enforced end‑to‑end; strong TLS
  • [ ] No PHI in logs; keys masked
  • [ ] Keys rotated; least‑privilege access

docs.title: Help
docs.text:
• Webhooks
  • Send webhooks
  • Verify callbacks

Quick tunnel

For demos only: start a quick tunnel in Admin → Tools → Tunnels, then set PUBLIC_API_URL. Replace with a permanent domain for production.

docs.title: Phaxio Go‑Live Checklist
docs.text:
Phaxio is a cloud backend where the provider fetches your PDF via a tokenized URL and posts status callbacks. This page covers the essentials to move from eval to production.

Quick links

Phaxio Webhooks Verify Callbacks Setup Wizard

docs.title: Pre‑flight
docs.text:
• [ ] Phaxio account and API credentials (PHAXIO_API_KEY, PHAXIO_API_SECRET)
  • [ ] Admin Console → Settings → Backend: phaxio
  • [ ] Set API_KEY and ensure clients send X-API-Key
  • [ ] PUBLIC_API_URL points to an HTTPS host reachable by Phaxio
  • [ ] Callback URL in Phaxio console set to <PUBLIC_API_URL>/phaxio-callback
  • [ ] HMAC verification enabled (PHAXIO_VERIFY_SIGNATURE=true)
  • [ ] Storage policy reviewed (HIPAA: disable provider document retention)
  • [ ] BAA executed (HIPAA)

Fast path with a tunnel (non‑HIPAA)

Use Admin Console → Tools → Tunnels to start a Cloudflare Quick Tunnel, then copy the public URL into PUBLIC_API_URL. Set the status callback URL in Phaxio to <PUBLIC_API_URL>/phaxio-callback. When you move to production, replace the quick tunnel with a permanent HTTPS domain.

docs.title: Webhooks
docs.text:
• Outbound status: POST /phaxio-callback
  • Inbound receiving (optional): POST /phaxio-inbound
  • Verification: HMAC (header X-Phaxio-Signature)

Helpful docs - Phaxio Webhooks (v2.1) - Send fax webhooks - Receive fax webhooks - Verifying callback requests

docs.title: Smoke tests
docs.text:
Send a test fax via Faxbot

curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -F "to=+15551234567" \
  -F "file=@README.md" | jq .

Poll job status (replace {job_id})

curl -sS "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax/{job_id}" -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY}" | jq .

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• 401 from Faxbot → set API_KEY and send X-API-Key
  • Callback not received → verify PUBLIC_API_URL is HTTPS and reachable; confirm <PUBLIC_API_URL>/phaxio-callback in Phaxio console
  • Signature mismatch → ensure PHAXIO_VERIFY_SIGNATURE=true and use the correct secret
  • 415 / file errors → only PDF/TXT are accepted; convert images to PDF first

HIPAA posture

Do not use quick tunnels for PHI. Use a permanent HTTPS domain, disable provider document retention, execute a BAA, and review HIPAA Requirements.

docs.title: SignalWire Go‑Live Checklist
docs.text:
SignalWire’s Compatibility (Twilio‑style) Fax API is supported for outbound sending. Faxbot hosts tokenized media for provider fetch and receives status callbacks. Inbound delivery is supported via the Plugin Manager (manifest providers) — install a SignalWire inbound plugin to enable full inbound flows.

Quick links

SignalWire Fax Compatibility API Plugin Builder

docs.title: Pre‑flight
docs.text:
• [ ] SignalWire Space URL (e.g., example.signalwire.com)
  • [ ] SignalWire Project ID and API Token
  • [ ] Optional From number (SIGNALWIRE_FAX_FROM_E164)
  • [ ] Admin Console → Settings → Backend: signalwire
  • [ ] PUBLIC_API_URL points to an HTTPS host reachable by SignalWire
  • [ ] Status callback configured to <PUBLIC_API_URL>/signalwire-callback
  • [ ] Inbound: after installing the SignalWire inbound plugin, copy the inbound webhook URL from Admin → Inbound and register it in your SignalWire console
  • [ ] Set API_KEY and ensure clients send X-API-Key

Quick tunnels for evaluation

Use Admin Console → Tools → Tunnels to create a temporary public URL (non‑HIPAA) and set PUBLIC_API_URL. Replace with a permanent domain for production.

docs.title: Webhooks
docs.text:
• Outbound status: POST /signalwire-callback
  • Inbound: plugin‑defined webhook path shown in Admin → Inbound after installation

Helpful docs - SignalWire — Fax - SignalWire — Compatibility API

docs.title: Smoke tests
docs.text:
Send a test fax via Faxbot

curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -F "to=+15551234567" \
  -F "file=@README.md" | jq .

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• Callback not received → verify PUBLIC_API_URL is HTTPS and reachable; confirm <PUBLIC_API_URL>/signalwire-callback is set
  • 415 / file errors → only PDF/TXT are accepted; convert images to PDF first
  • Inbound not delivering → ensure the SignalWire inbound plugin is installed/enabled and that you registered the exact inbound URL shown in Admin → Inbound

HIPAA posture

Do not use quick tunnels for PHI. Use a permanent HTTPS domain and follow HIPAA Requirements.

See also - Plugin Builder

docs.title: Sinch Go‑Live — One‑Pager
docs.text: Direct upload with Fax API v3.

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] SINCH_PROJECT_ID, SINCH_API_KEY, SINCH_API_SECRET
  • [ ] Admin Console → Backend: sinch
  • [ ] Optional: SINCH_AUTH_METHOD=oauth (HIPAA‑aligned)
  • [ ] Inbound (optional): <PUBLIC_API_URL>/sinch-inbound + Basic auth
  • [ ] API_KEY minted; clients send X-API-Key
  • [ ] PUBLIC_API_URL set for Admin links

docs.title: Test
docs.text:
curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -F to=+15551234567 -F file=@README.md | jq .

docs.title: Help
docs.text:
• Sinch Fax API — Reference
  • Access Keys (Dashboard)

docs.title: HIPAA checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Prefer OAuth (SINCH_AUTH_METHOD=oauth)
  • [ ] HTTPS enforced; strong TLS
  • [ ] No PHI in logs; keys masked
  • [ ] Keys rotated; least‑privilege access

HIPAA

Prefer OAuth, enforce HTTPS, and review HIPAA Requirements.

docs.title: Sinch Go‑Live Checklist (Fax API v3)
docs.text:
Sinch Fax API v3 uses direct upload: Faxbot posts your file to Sinch and maps the immediate response. PUBLIC_API_URL is not required for outbound, but is recommended for Admin/Diagnostics and inbound.

Quick links

Fax API Reference Access Keys Setup Wizard

docs.title: Pre‑flight
docs.text:
• [ ] Sinch project and access keys (Dashboard → Settings → Access Keys)
  • [ ] Environment: SINCH_PROJECT_ID, SINCH_API_KEY, SINCH_API_SECRET
  • [ ] Optional: SINCH_AUTH_METHOD=oauth for HIPAA‑aligned auth; set SINCH_AUTH_BASE_URL for your region if needed
  • [ ] Admin Console → Settings → Backend: sinch
  • [ ] Inbound (optional): configure provider to POST /sinch-inbound and enable Basic auth (SINCH_INBOUND_BASIC_USER, SINCH_INBOUND_BASIC_PASS)
  • [ ] Set API_KEY and ensure clients send X-API-Key
  • [ ] PUBLIC_API_URL set for Admin links and inbound flows

Where to find keys

Use the Sinch Customer (Build) Dashboard: dashboard.sinch.com/settings/access-keys. Other Sinch portals may not show Fax API access keys.

docs.title: Webhooks
docs.text:
• Outbound status: mapped from send response today; additional callbacks may be added later
  • Inbound receiving (optional): POST /sinch-inbound
  • Verification: Basic auth (set SINCH_INBOUND_BASIC_USER/PASS) and use IP allowlists at your edge

Helpful docs - Sinch Fax API — Reference

docs.title: Smoke tests
docs.text:
Send a test fax via Faxbot

curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -F "to=+15551234567" \
  -F "file=@README.md" | jq .

Poll job status (replace {job_id})

curl -sS "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax/{job_id}" -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY}" | jq .

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• 401 from Faxbot → set API_KEY and send X-API-Key
  • 401 from provider → verify Project ID and key/secret; check region and auth method
  • Inbound rejected → ensure Basic auth credentials set and applied at your endpoint; prefer application/json payloads

HIPAA posture

Prefer OAuth (SINCH_AUTH_METHOD=oauth) and ensure your ingress is HTTPS with strict TLS. Review HIPAA Requirements.

docs.title: SIP/Asterisk — One‑Pager
docs.text: Self‑hosted telephony with Asterisk.

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Trunk registered; NAT/TLS configured
  • [ ] T.38 enabled (or G.711 fallback)
  • [ ] AMI credentials set in Admin
  • [ ] Admin Console → Backend: sip
  • [ ] Inbound enabled and storage configured
  • [ ] PUBLIC_API_URL set for Admin links

docs.title: Test
docs.text:
• Send a test fax via your trunk
  • Simulate inbound to verify storage and Inbox
curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/admin/inbound/simulate" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"backend":"sip","fr":"+15559876543","to":"+15551234567","pages":1}' | jq .

docs.title: Help
docs.text:
• SIP Setup (Faxbot)
  • Asterisk Project

HIPAA

Keep telephony private. Use WireGuard/Tailscale for Admin only and review HIPAA Requirements.

docs.title: SIP/Asterisk Go‑Live Checklist
docs.text: Self‑hosted backend using Asterisk. Requires proper SIP trunking, NAT, TLS, and T.38 where possible. Inbound delivery is internal; no external provider webhooks.

docs.title: Pre‑flight
docs.text:
• [ ] Asterisk reachable and stable; SIP trunk provisioned
  • [ ] T.38 enabled end‑to‑end where supported; fall back to G.711 pass‑through if needed
  • [ ] AMI credentials configured (Faxbot requires AMI; see Settings)
  • [ ] Admin Console → Settings → Backend: sip
  • [ ] Inbound enabled and storage configured for inbound PDFs
  • [ ] PUBLIC_API_URL set for Admin links (not required for telephony)
  • [ ] Ports/firewall hardened; TLS on signaling preferred

Tunnels

Don’t expose SIP/UDPTL over public tunnels. If you need remote Admin access, tunnel only the HTTPS API (8080) and keep telephony private.

docs.title: Inbound
docs.text:
• Internal route: POST /_internal/asterisk/inbound (used by your Asterisk dialplan)
  • Inbound PDFs are stored via the configured storage backend and visible in Inbox

Helpful docs - SIP Setup (Faxbot) - Asterisk Project

docs.title: Smoke tests
docs.text:
• Place a test outbound fax through your trunk
  • Post a simulated inbound to validate storage and Inbox
curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/admin/inbound/simulate" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"backend":"sip","fr":"+15559876543","to":"+15551234567","pages":1}' | jq .

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• One‑way audio or failure → verify NAT/ALG, T.38 negotiation, and trunk codecs
  • Jobs stuck → check AMI connectivity and Asterisk logs
  • Inbound missing → confirm dialplan posts to /_internal/asterisk/inbound and storage backend is healthy

HIPAA posture

Use WireGuard/Tailscale for remote access; never expose AMI or SIP to the public internet. Review HIPAA Requirements.

docs.title: Networking & Tunnels
docs.text:
Options to reach your Faxbot API from outside your LAN. Use a quick tunnel for demos, and a HIPAA‑capable tunnel for production.

Quick links

Setup Wizard Diagnostics Webhooks

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• Cloudflare Quick Tunnel (non‑HIPAA): easiest way to test webhooks and the Admin Console over the internet.
  • WireGuard or Tailscale (HIPAA‑capable): private, durable connectivity for Admin/iOS; keep SIP/UDPTL private.
  • Public HTTPS domain: for production, terminate TLS and expose only the API port.

Admin Console helper

Admin → Tools → Tunnels lets you start/stop quick tunnels, tail logs, and auto‑detect your public URL. The Admin also renders the best base URL for mobile pairing.

docs.title: Quick Tunnel (Cloudflare)
docs.text: Use for evaluations only (no PHI). The Admin can discover the https://*.trycloudflare.com URL and set PUBLIC_API_URL. Replace with a permanent hostname before go‑live.

docs.title: WireGuard / Tailscale
docs.text:
Use a private tunnel for production, especially with PHI or self‑hosted backends (SIP/Asterisk/FreeSWITCH).

Checklist - [ ] Do not expose SIP/UDPTL publicly - [ ] Expose only HTTPS API (8080) - [ ] Rate limit and enforce strong TLS - [ ] Verify mobile pairing and Admin access through the tunnel

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• Webhook not reaching → verify PUBLIC_API_URL is set and reachable over HTTPS
  • Quick tunnel frequently changes URL → providers may reject it; use a named tunnel or domain
  • Mobile pairing fails → ensure the best base URL resolves on device and that the tunnel is running

See also - Webhooks - Admin Console: Diagnostics

docs.title: Operations
docs.text: Runbooks and practices for stable deployments.

docs.title: Monitoring
docs.text:
• Health endpoint and Admin Diagnostics
  • Provider status mapping and job retries

docs.title: Backups
docs.text:
• DB snapshots and storage backups
  • Key rotation schedule

docs.title: Incidents
docs.text:
• Use Admin → Jobs and Inbox to localize issues
  • Check provider dashboards and webhook reachability

See also - Troubleshooting - Security

docs.title: Reference
docs.text:
Links to API reference and SDKs.

  • REST API: Swagger · OpenAPI JSON
  • SDKs: SDKS.md
  • MCP tools and transports: see Admin → Diagnostics and repo docs

Try the explorer

Use Swagger or Redoc to explore endpoints and schemas. Keep X-API-Key handy for local testing.

docs.title: Security
docs.text: Secure defaults for keys, webhooks, storage, and Admin access.

docs.title: Keys
docs.text:
• Use API Keys with scopes; rotate regularly
  • Prefer OAuth/OIDC for Admin login where available

docs.title: Webhooks
docs.text:
• Enforce HMAC where supported (Phaxio, HumbleFax)
  • Use Basic auth for providers without signing (Sinch inbound)
  • Always use HTTPS

docs.title: Storage
docs.text:
• Tokenized, short‑TTL links for PDFs
  • S3/compatible storage supported; avoid public buckets

docs.title: HIPAA
docs.text:
• Execute BAA with cloud providers
  • Disable provider document retention
  • Avoid quick tunnels; use private tunnels and a permanent HTTPS domain

See also - HIPAA Requirements - Networking & Tunnels

docs.title: Network Hardening
docs.text: Lock down your deployment for production and PHI.

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Expose only HTTPS API port (8080)
  • [ ] Keep SIP/UDPTL private (self‑hosted)
  • [ ] Rate limit and WAF at edge
  • [ ] Disable Admin Terminal over public ingress
  • [ ] Use WireGuard/Tailscale for remote Admin access

See also - Networking & Tunnels

docs.title: OAuth / OIDC Setup
docs.text: Protect the Admin Console with your identity provider.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1) Create an OAuth/OIDC app in your IdP (Auth0/Okta/etc.) 2) Configure Redirect URI to your Admin domain 3) Enter client id/secret in Admin → Settings → Security 4) Test login from a private window

Scopes and claims

Map Admin roles from IdP claims if available. Keep Admin access limited to operators.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• Invalid redirect → verify exact URI and HTTPS
  • Forbidden → check user group/role mapping

docs.title: Phaxio Setup
docs.text:
Cloud backend where Phaxio fetches your PDF via a tokenized URL and posts status callbacks.

Quick links

Webhooks Verify Callbacks Diagnostics

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1) Admin Console → Setup Wizard → choose Phaxio 2) Enter PHAXIO_API_KEY and PHAXIO_API_SECRET 3) Set PUBLIC_API_URL (HTTPS) so Phaxio can reach your callbacks and tokenized PDFs 4) In Phaxio console, set status callback URL to <PUBLIC_API_URL>/phaxio-callback 5) Enable HMAC verification: PHAXIO_VERIFY_SIGNATURE=true

Evaluating without a domain?

Use Admin → Tools → Tunnels to create a Cloudflare Quick Tunnel (non‑HIPAA) and copy the public URL into PUBLIC_API_URL. Replace with a permanent HTTPS domain for production.

docs.title: Webhooks
docs.text:
• Status callback: POST /phaxio-callback
  • Inbound receiving: POST /phaxio-inbound
  • Verification: HMAC (header X-Phaxio-Signature)

Helpful links - Webhooks overview - Send webhooks - Receive webhooks - Verify callbacks

docs.title: Quick test
docs.text:
curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -F to=+15551234567 -F file=@README.md | jq .

HIPAA posture

Execute a BAA, disable provider document retention, and use a permanent HTTPS domain. Avoid quick tunnels for PHI. See HIPAA Requirements.

docs.title: SignalWire Setup (Compatibility API)
docs.text:
Outbound fax via SignalWire’s Compatibility (Twilio‑style) API. Inbound delivery is supported via the Plugin Manager (manifest providers), so you can enable full inbound flows when you install or upload a SignalWire inbound plugin.

Quick links

SignalWire Fax Compatibility API Plugin Builder

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1) Collect credentials: Space URL (e.g., example.signalwire.com), Project ID, API Token 2) Admin Console → Setup Wizard → choose SignalWire 3) Enter values and optional SIGNALWIRE_FAX_FROM_E164 4) Set PUBLIC_API_URL (HTTPS) for tokenized media 5) Configure status callback to <PUBLIC_API_URL>/signalwire-callback 6) For inbound: install or upload a SignalWire inbound plugin in Admin → Plugins. The Admin Console will display the exact inbound webhook URL (copy‑to‑clipboard) for you to register in your SignalWire project.

Helpful links - SignalWire — Fax - Compatibility API

docs.title: Quick test (outbound)
docs.text:
curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -F to=+15551234567 -F file=@README.md | jq .

docs.title: Inbound (via Plugin Manager)
docs.text:
• Use Admin → Plugin Builder (or Manifest Upload) to add an inbound provider for SignalWire. The inbound plugin defines the webhook path and verification method (e.g., HMAC or Basic auth) and surfaces the callback URL in Admin → Inbound.
  • After installation, register the displayed inbound webhook URL in your SignalWire console. Validate delivery in Admin → Inbox.

Helpful link - Plugin Builder

HIPAA posture

Use a permanent HTTPS domain and avoid quick tunnels for PHI. See HIPAA Requirements.

docs.title: Sinch Setup (Fax API v3)
docs.text:
Direct upload backend: Faxbot posts PDFs to Sinch; PUBLIC_API_URL not required for outbound.

Quick links

API Reference Access Keys Diagnostics

docs.title: Steps
docs.text: 1) Get Sinch keys: dashboard.sinch.com/settings/access-keys 2) Admin Console → Setup Wizard → choose Sinch 3) Enter SINCH_PROJECT_ID, SINCH_API_KEY, SINCH_API_SECRET 4) Optional for HIPAA: SINCH_AUTH_METHOD=oauth (and SINCH_AUTH_BASE_URL for your region) 5) Optional inbound: set <PUBLIC_API_URL>/sinch-inbound and enforce Basic auth

docs.title: Webhooks
docs.text:
• Outbound: mapped from send response (callbacks may be added later)
  • Inbound: POST /sinch-inbound
  • Verification: Basic auth headers + IP allowlists

Helpful link - Sinch Fax API — Reference

docs.title: Quick test
docs.text:
curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/fax" \
  -H "X-API-Key: ${API_KEY:?}" \
  -F to=+15551234567 -F file=@README.md | jq .

HIPAA posture

Prefer OAuth for outbound auth, enforce HTTPS, and avoid quick tunnels for PHI. See HIPAA Requirements.

docs.title: Webhooks
docs.text:
Configure provider callbacks for outbound status and inbound fax delivery. The Admin Console shows exact URLs for your active providers and verifies reachability.

Quick links

Networking & Tunnels Diagnostics Plugin Builder

docs.title: Outbound status
docs.text:
• Phaxio: POST /phaxio-callback (HMAC verification)
  • SignalWire: POST /signalwire-callback
  • Sinch: status mapped from send response; additional callbacks may be introduced by plugins

docs.title: Inbound delivery
docs.text:
• Phaxio: POST /phaxio-inbound (HMAC verification)
  • Sinch: POST /sinch-inbound (use Basic auth; add IP allowlists)
  • HumbleFax: POST /inbound/humblefax/webhook (HMAC‑SHA256 when configured)
  • SignalWire: via Plugin Manager — install or upload a SignalWire inbound plugin; the Admin will display the plugin‑defined inbound path

Find your URLs

Admin → Inbound shows the active inbound provider, verification method (HMAC/Basic), and a copy‑to‑clipboard webhook URL. Admin → Settings → Backend lists outbound status callbacks.

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
• Use HTTPS URLs
  • Enable HMAC or Basic auth where supported
  • Avoid quick tunnels for PHI; prefer named tunnels or a domain

docs.title: Test quickly
docs.text:
curl -sS -X POST "$PUBLIC_API_URL/phaxio-callback" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"test":true}'

Use Admin → Diagnostics to run provider‑specific checks.

See also - Networking & Tunnels - Go‑Live Checklists

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Common issues and quick fixes.

docs.title: Outbound
docs.text:
• 401 from API → set API_KEY and send X-API-Key
  • 413 / 415 → adjust MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB; only PDF/TXT supported
  • Callback not received → verify <PUBLIC_API_URL> (HTTPS) and correct callback path in provider console

docs.title: Inbound
docs.text:
• No inbound PDFs → confirm storage configured and webhook verification matches provider
  • Sinch inbound 401 → ensure Basic auth credentials set and used
  • Plugin inbound not delivering → verify plugin installed/enabled and URL registered

docs.title: Tunnels
docs.text:
• Quick tunnel unstable → use a named tunnel or domain for production
  • Mobile pairing fails → ensure best base URL resolves on device

See also - Diagnostics - Networking & Tunnels
